title: [CI:DOCS] update auto-update docs
body:
Rewrite the auto-update man page.  It was quite dusty and out-dated as it was not mentioning Quadlet at all.  At times it was too verbose about internal implementation details that users shouldn't need to worry about.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Refresh of the podman-auto-update man page and documentation.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Is this a one off or is CI bottlenecked at the moment. `53 minutes ago` and still not finished for a docs PR seems unusually long. Cc: @cevich
Job was waiting on Win Installer, which just started two minutes ago.

> Job was waiting on Win Installer, which just started two minutes ago.

IIRC, Jason helped me out by moving the windows build job from a cirrus-container to a VM.  I didn't look at the sequence, but in theory that would slow down the entire windows task-chain.

OK, thanks!  Probably something should keep an eye on.

CI is green and ready to merge :checkered_flag:

Yes please and thanks @vrothberg.  Windows is (in general) a bit of a black-box for this crowd.  It's "style" of error reporting leaves a lot to be desired, and it's it's easy to mistrust given it's history.  Regardless of cirrus/github shenanigans, I find it's a good policy to carefully observe Windows only out the corner of your non-dominant eye.  A prudent "safety-squint" to protect against going completely blind :wink:
